description_en: An ichirizuka (a traditional distance-marker mound), in the historic site subcategory of the monuments category. It is town-designated by Nanbu. The designation was made on 1996-05-29.
The thirteen mounds (jūsanzuka) of Yagita, in the historic site subcategory of the monuments category. It is town-designated by Nanbu. The designation was made on 2016-05-18.
The ginkgo tree (ichō) of Sankō-ji temple, designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is town-designated by Nanbu. The designation was made on 1974-07-22.
The Japanese yew (onko) of Shōju-ji temple, designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is town-designated by Nanbu. The designation was made on 1974-07-22.
The Akaishi zelkova (keyaki) tree, designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is town-designated by Nanbu. The designation was made on 1974-07-22.
A weeping chestnut (shidare kuri) tree, designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is town-designated by Nanbu. The designation was made on 1976-03-26.
The Married Couple Cedar, a twin-trunk cedar (futamata sugi), designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is town-designated by Nanbu. The designation was made on 1979-07-10.
The great cedar (ōsugi) of Hachiman, designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is town-designated by Nanbu. The designation was made on 1980-03-26.
A fir tree (momi no ki) known as the Topico Tree, designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is town-designated by Nanbu. The designation was made on 2009-11-18.
The umbrella pine (kasamatsu) of Isesawa, designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is town-designated by Nanbu. The designation was made on 2009-11-18.
The pine tree associated with the mountain deity (Yama no Kami), designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is town-designated by Nanbu. The designation was made on 2009-11-18.
The Higakubo tiger dance (toramai), an intangible folk performing art within the folk cultural properties category. It is town-designated by Oirase.
The Honmura rooster dance (torimai), an intangible folk performing art within the folk cultural properties category. It is town-designated by Oirase.
The Honmura lion dance (shishimai), an intangible folk performing art within the folk cultural properties category. It is town-designated by Oirase.
A ginkgo tree (ichō) of approximately 800 years in age, designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is town-designated by Tōhoku.
A ginkgo tree (ichō) of approximately 650 years in age, designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is town-designated by Tōhoku.

name_en: Monument of the Center of Japan
description_en: The monument known as Nihon Chūō no Hi (Monument of the Center of Japan), classified within the tangible cultural properties category. It is town-designated by Tōhoku, located at Ie-no-Shitada, Tōhoku, Kamikita District, Aomori Prefecture.

name_romaji: Kyū Tsuruta-chō Ritsu Mizumoto Shōgakkō Kōsha (Tsuruta-machi Rekishi Bunka Denshōkan)
name_en: Former Tsuruta Municipal Mizumoto Elementary School Building (Tsuruta History and Culture Heritage Hall)
description_en: The former Tsuruta municipal Mizumoto Elementary School building, now the Tsuruta History and Culture Heritage Hall, classified within the tangible cultural properties category. It is town-designated by Tsuruta, located at Myōdōzaki, Tsuruta, Kitatsugaru District.
